summary: Token overhead comparisons of coding agents drive optimization; Manus acquisition geopolitics; user adoption signals from Terence Tao and enterprise discussions. | Official vendor signals: Anthropic containment, Mistral Vibe, JetBrains benchmark, Google ADK 2.0, OpenAI GPT-5.6 integration, Daytona funding, Supabase agentic coding, Fly.io agent practices, and infra primitives. | Agent Radar OSS screening: fragmented orchestration tooling (ccmux, wmux, h5i) and security/infra primitives (agent-opfor, Armorer, token-efficient-agent-harness-lab) dominate new repos, while vendor releases from Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, Cline, OpenHands, and Qwen show steady churn. BitFun (1.3k stars) stands out as a desktop-grade agent runtime but lacks fresh user workflow evidence. Social/discussion coverage is missing entirely. | Package ecosystem shows strong activity in agent observability, security, and memory tooling. OpenAI Codex and GitHub Copilot CLI updates lead mainstream product signals. Caracal SDK series introduces policy-based agent authority. Multiple eval frameworks and agent detection libraries emerge.
